hypothesis: Systems-optimizer ratchets become safer if every writable_scope entry must stay normalized and repo-relative, so dot-segment or separator escapes cannot masquerade as allowlisted control-plane paths.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` matched raw writable_scope globs against the allowlist, so malformed entries like `CLAW/scripts/../control-plane/runtime/**` or `CLAW/scripts/../../site/src/app/**` could still inherit the allowed `CLAW/scripts/**` prefix and verify clean. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any writable_scope entry that contains dot-segment traversal, duplicate slash separators, a leading slash, or backslash separators, while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails non-normalized writable_scope entries that try to escape through dot segments or separator drift and the fixed evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Systems-optimizer writable_scope entries must stay normalized repo-relative patterns; raw allowlist matching alone can hide dot-segment escapes into runtime or route surfaces.
